sentences.words: 教育与经济发展之间，到底是什么关系？

sentences.id: We have to suspect that continuing economic growth promotes the development of education even when governments don’t force it. (19 words)
sentences.en: 我们不得不怀疑，即使政府不强制发展教育，经济持续增长也会促进教育发展。
sentences.cn: After all, that’s how education got started. (7 words)
sentences.words: 毕竟，教育就是这样产生的。

sentences.id: When our ancestors were hunters and gatherers 10,000 years ago, they didn’t have time to wonder much about anything besides finding food. (22 words)
sentences.en: 一万年前当我们的祖先还在过着采集狩猎的生活之时，他们根本没有时间去思考发现食物以外的其他任何事情。
sentences.cn: Only when humanity began to get its food in a more productive way was there time for other things. (19 words)
sentences.words: 只有当人类能够更高效地获取食物，他们才有时间做其它的事情。

sentences.id: As education improved, humanity’s productivity potential increased as well. (9 words)
sentences.en: 教育一发展，人类生产潜力也会提高。
sentences.cn: When the competitive environment pushed our ancestors to achieve that potential, they could in turn afford more education. (18 words)
sentences.words: 当竞争环境促使我们的祖先实现这一生产潜力，他们反过来又有能力获得更多的教育机会。

sentences.id: This increasingly high level of education is probably a necessary, but not a sufficient, condition for the complex political systems required by advanced economic performance. (25 words)
sentences.en: 先进的经济水平要求复杂的政治制度，越来越高的教育水平可能是这种复杂政治制度必要的，但不是充分的条件。
sentences.cn: Thus poor countries might not be able to escape their poverty traps without political changes that may be possible only with broader formal education. (24 words)
sentences.words: 因此，政治改革才可以使贫穷国家摆脱贫困陷阱，而政治改革则只能靠更广泛的正规教育实现。

sentences.id: A lack of formal education, however, doesn’t constrain the ability of the developing world’s workforce to substantially improve productivity for the foreseeable future. (23 words)
sentences.en: 但是， 发展中国家的劳动力在可预见的未来充分提高生产力的能力没有因缺乏正规教育而受到限制。
sentences.cn: On the contrary, constraints on improving productivity explain why education isn’t developing more quickly there than it is. (18 words)
sentences.words: 相反，正是因为生产力的提高受到了各种限制，所以这些国家的教育没能更快地发展。

sentences.id:
0904
The most thoroughly studied intellectuals in the history of the New World are the ministers and political leaders of seventeenth-century New England. (22 words)
sentences.en: 新大陆史上被研究得最彻底的知识分子是17世纪新英格兰的牧师和政治领袖。
sentences.cn: According to the standard history of American philosophy, nowhere else in colonial America was “so much importance attached to intellectual pursuits.” (21 words)
sentences.words: 根据美国标准哲学史的记载，在美洲殖民地中，没有任何地方能“如此重视知识的追求。”

sentences.id: According to many books and articles, New England’s leaders established the basic themes and preoccupations of an unfolding, dominant Puritan tradition in American intellectual life. (25 words)
sentences.en: 据大量书籍和文献记载，清教传统正日益发展，逐渐占据统治地位，新英格兰的领袖确立了美国精神生活中清教传统的基本主旨和要点。
sentences.cn: To take this approach to the New Englanders normally means to start with the Puritans’ theological innovations and their distinctive ideas about the church—important subjects that we may not neglect. (31 words)
sentences.words: 通过这条途径来了解新英格兰人，通常意味着要首先研究清教徒的神学创新及其对于教会的独特看法——这些是我们不可忽略的重要课题。

sentences.id: But in keeping with our examination of southern intellectual life, we may consider the original Puritans as carriers of European culture, adjusting to New World circumstances. (26 words)
sentences.en: 但是为了与我们对南部精神生活的考察保持一致，我们可以认为最初的清教徒们带来了欧洲文化，他们根据新大陆的情况进行了调整。
sentences.cn: The New England colonies were the scenes of important episodes in the pursuit of widely understood ideals of civility and virtuosity. (21 words)
sentences.words: 在追求公认的文明礼节和精湛技能的理想过程当中，新英格兰殖民地发生了许多重要的事情。

sentences.id: The early settlers of Massachusetts Bay included men of impressive education and influence in England. (15 words)
sentences.en: 马萨诸塞湾的早期移民就包括了那些在英国接受过良好的教育并深具影响力的人。
sentences.cn: Besides the ninety or so learned ministers who came to Massachusetts churches in the decade after 1629, there were political leaders like John Winthrop, an educated gentleman, lawyer, and official of the Crown before he journeyed to Boston. (38 words)
sentences.words: 在1629年之后的十年间，除了来到马萨诸塞教会的约90位博学的牧师，还有像约翰·温斯罗普（John Winthrop）这样的政治领袖，他是一位绅士、律师，受过良好教育，到波士顿之前曾是皇室官员。

sentences.id: These men wrote and published extensively, reaching both New World and Old World audiences, and giving New England an atmosphere of intellectual earnestness. (23 words)
sentences.en: 这些人著作等身，读者遍及新旧大陆，这给新英格兰营造了一种求知若渴的氛围。
sentences.cn: We should not forget, however, that most New Englanders were less well educated. (13 words)
sentences.words: 但是，我们不该忘记，大多数新英格兰人并未受过良好的教育。

sentences.id: While few craftsmen or farmers, let alone dependents and servants, left literary compositions to be analyzed, it is obvious that their views were less fully intellectualized. (26 words)
sentences.en: 虽然鲜有工匠或农民，更别提其子女和仆人，留下只言片语可供分析；但是很明显他们的观点并不具有太大的思想性。
sentences.cn: Their thinking often had a traditional superstitious quality. (8 words)
sentences.words: 他们的思想中往往带有一种传统的迷信色彩。

sentences.id: A tailor named John Dane, who emigrated in the late 1630s, left an account of his reasons for leaving England that is filled with signs. (25 words)
sentences.en: 有个裁缝，叫约翰·戴恩（John Dane），于17世纪30年代末期移居新大陆，他留下一个记录，陈述了离开英国的理由，其内容充满了各种符号征兆。
sentences.cn: Sexual confusion, economic frustrations, and religious hope—all came together in a decisive moment when he opened the Bible, told his father that the first line he saw would settle his fate, and read the magical words: “Come out from among them, touch no unclean thing, and I will be your God and you shall be my people.” (58 words)
sentences.words: 他翻开《圣经》，告诉父亲，他所见的第一行字会决定他的命运，然后就读到了以下神奇的文字：“从他们中间走出来，不要沾不洁净的物，我将成为你们的神，你将是我的子民。”在这一决定性的时刻，性方面的困感、经济上的挫败、还有宗教上期待——所有的一切都一股脑儿涌现出来。

sentences.id: One wonders what Dane thought of the careful sermons explaining the Bible that he heard in Puritan churches. (18 words)
sentences.en: 人们会纳闷，戴恩在清教徒教堂里听到那些精心诠释圣经的布道后会做何感想。
sentences.cn: Meanwhile, many settlers had slighter religious commitments than Dane’s, as one clergyman learned in confronting folk along the coast who mocked that they had not come to the New World for religion. (32 words)
sentences.words: 同时，许多移民并没有戴恩那般虔诚的宗教信仰，就像一位牧师在海边遇到渔夫听到他们说的那样，那些人嘲弄说自己来新大陆可不是为了宗教。

sentences.id: “Our main end was to catch fish.” (7 words)
sentences.en: “ 我们最终目的是来捕鱼。”
sentences.cn:
1001
Of all the changes that have taken place in English-language newspapers during the past quarter-century, perhaps the most far-reaching has been the inexorable decline in the scope and seriousness of their arts coverage. (33 words)
sentences.words: 过去的25年，英文报纸发生的所有变化中，影响最深远的就是其艺术报道范围和严肃性呈现不可遏止的衰落之势。

sentences.id: It is difficult to the point of impossibility for the average reader under the age of forty to imagine a time when high-quality arts criticism could be found in most big-city newspapers. (32 words)
sentences.en: 对于40岁以下的普通读者而言，他们很难甚至不可能想象得到，当年还可以在许多大城市报纸上读到高质量的艺术评论。
sentences.cn: Yet a considerable number of the most significant collections of criticism published in the 20th century consisted in large part of newspaper reviews. (23 words)
sentences.words: 然而在20世纪出版的大量最重要的评论集中，很大部分都是报刊评论。

sentences.id: To read such books today is to marvel at the fact that their learned contents were once deemed suitable for publication in general-circulation dailies. (24 words)
sentences.en: 若今天再读这些书，定会大为震惊：文集中这些精深的内容竟然当成是适合发表在大众日报中的文章。
sentences.cn: We are even farther removed from the unfocused newspaper reviews published in England between the turn of the 20th century and the eve of World War Ⅱ, at a time when newsprint was dirt-cheap and stylish arts criticism was considered an ornament to the publications in which it appeared. (49 words)
sentences.words: 甚至更让我们不解的还有，从20世纪伊始到二战前夕英国报纸上评论文章主题广泛，包罗万象。当时新闻纸相当便宜，人们认为时髦的艺术评论是其刊载出版物的一个点缀。

sentences.id: In those far-off days, it was taken for granted that the critics of major papers would write in detail and at length about the events they covered. (27 words)
sentences.en: 在那些遥远的年代，各大报刊的评论家都会做长篇大论详细评论他们所报道的事情，这在当时看做是理所当然的。
sentences.cn: Theirs was a serious business, and even those reviewers who wore their learning lightly, like George Bernard Shaw and Ernest Newman, could be trusted to know what they were about. (30 words)
sentences.words: 他们干的是正事，人们相信：即使是萧伯纳和欧内斯特·纽曼这样不掉书袋博学的评论家，他们也知道自己要干啥。

sentences.id: These men believed in journalism as a calling, and were proud to be published in the daily press. (18 words)
sentences.en: 这帮人奉新闻业为天职，并且对自己的文章能见诸于报刊颇为自豪。
sentences.cn: “So few authors have brains enough or literary gift enough to keep their own end up in journalism,” Newman wrote, “that I am tempted to define ‘journalism’ as ‘a term of contempt applied by writers who are not read to writers who are’.” (43 words)
sentences.words: “能拥有足够智慧或文学天赋在新闻写作中坚持不懈的人少之又少”，纽曼曾写道,“以至于我很想把‘新闻写作’定义为不受读者欢迎的作家用来嘲讽受读者欢迎的作家的一个 ‘轻蔑之词’ ”。

sentences.id: Unfortunately, these critics are virtually forgotten. (6 words)
sentences.en: 不幸的是，这些评论家实际上已经被人遗忘。
sentences.cn: Neville Cardus, who wrote for the Manchester Guardian from 1917 until shortly before his death in 1975, is now known solely as a writer of essays on the game of cricket. (31 words)
sentences.words: 内维尔·卡达斯，从1917年到1975年去世前不久，一直都在为《曼彻斯特卫报》写文章，如今人们仅仅认为他只是个写板球比赛文章的作家。

sentences.id: During his lifetime, though, he was also one of England’s foremost classical-music critics, and a stylist so widely admired that his Autobiography (1947) became a best-seller. (26 words)
sentences.en: 但在其一生中，他还是英国首屈一指的古典音乐评论家，也是一位广受读者尊崇的文体家，他的《自传》（1947年出版）就曾一时洛阳纸贵。
sentences.cn: He was knighted in 1967, the first music critic to be so honored. (13 words)
sentences.words: 1967年他获封为爵士，也是第一位获此殊荣的音乐评论家。

sentences.id: Yet only one of his books is now in print, and his vast body of writings on music is unknown save to specialists. (23 words)
sentences.en: 然而，他的书现在只有一本还在出版；大量的音乐评论，除了专业人士，已鲜为人知。
sentences.cn: Is there any chance that Cardus’s criticism will enjoy a revival? (11 words)
sentences.words: 卡达斯的艺术评论还有机会再度盛行吗？

sentences.id: The prospect seems remote. (4 words)
sentences.en: 前景似乎渺茫。
sentences.cn: Journalistic tastes had changed long before his death, and postmodern readers have little use for the richly upholstered Vicwardian prose in which he specialized. (24 words)
sentences.words: 在他去世之前，新闻工作者的喜好就早已改变，而且他擅长写那种文风华丽铺陈的维多爱德时期的散文，后现代读者觉得没什么益处。

sentences.id: Moreover, the amateur tradition in music criticism has been in headlong retreat. (12 words)
sentences.en: 更何况，业余爱好者来做音乐评论的传统早已成为昨日黄花了。
sentences.cn:
1002
Over the past decade, thousands of patents have been granted for what are called business methods. (16 words)
sentences.words: 过去的十年，有成千上万专利授予给了所谓的商业模式。

sentences.id: Amazon.com received one for its “one-click” online payment system. (9 words)
sentences.en: 亚马逊（Amazon.com）的“一键下单”在线支付系统就获得了一项专利。
sentences.cn: Merrill Lynch got legal protection for an asset allocation strategy. (10 words)
sentences.words: 美林证劵的资产配置策略得到了法律保护。

sentences.id: One inventor patented a technique for lifting a box. (9 words)
sentences.en: 有个发明人还为其搬箱子的技术获得了专利。
sentences.cn: Now the nation’s top patent court appears completely ready to scale back on business-method patents, which have been controversial ever since they were first authorized 10 years ago. (28 words)
sentences.words: 现在国家最高专利法院似乎做好了充分准备，要缩减商业模式专利，因其自从十年前首次批准以来就一直争议不断。

sentences.id: In a move that has intellectual-property lawyers abuzz, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit said it would use a particular case to conduct a broad review of business-method patents. (32 words)
sentences.en: 在一项提议使得知识产权律师议论纷纷，在这个提议中，美国联邦巡回上诉法院声称，它将利用一项特殊案件来对商业模式专利进行广泛的复审。
sentences.cn: In re Bilski, as the case is known, is “a very big deal,” says Dennis D. Crouch of the University of Missouri School of Law. (25 words)
sentences.words: 众所周知的比尔斯基案，就是“一个非常大的事件”，密苏里大学法学院的丹尼斯·D·克劳奇这样说道。

sentences.id: It “has the potential to eliminate an entire class of patents.” (11 words)
sentences.en: 它“很可能会导致整个专利类别的彻底取消”。
sentences.cn: Curbs on business-method claims would be a dramatic about-face, because it was the Federal Circuit itself that introduced such patents with its 1998 decision in the so-called State Street Bank case, approving a patent on a way of pooling mutual-fund assets. (41 words)
sentences.words: 限制商业模式申请专利将会戏剧性的变革，因为在1998年所谓的道富银行案件中，正是联邦巡回法院自己做出判决，批准对共同基金的筹资方式授予专利，由此引入了这类商业模式专利。

sentences.id: That ruling produced an explosion in business-method patent filings, initially by emerging Internet companies trying to stake out exclusive rights to specific types of online transactions. (26 words)
sentences.en: 这一裁决使得商业模式专利申请档案爆炸式增长，起初只是一些新兴的互联网公司，他们想要清楚地表明拥有特定类型的在线交易方式的独家权利。
sentences.cn: Later, more established companies raced to add such patents to their files, if only as a defensive move against rivals that might beat them to the punch. (27 words)
sentences.words: 后来，越来越多的老牌公司也竞相将此类专利入卷归档，至少作为防御措施，以防竞争对手抢占先机。

sentences.id: In 2005, IBM noted in a court filing that it had been issued more than 300 business-method patents, despite the fact that it questioned the legal basis for granting them. (30 words)
sentences.en: 2005年，IBM 公司在一份法院案卷中发现自己被授予了300多项商业模式专利，尽管连它自己也质疑授权专利的法律基础。
sentences.cn: Similarly, some Wall Street investment firms armed themselves with patents for financial products, even as they took positions in court cases opposing the practice. (24 words)
sentences.words: 无独有偶，华尔街一些投资公司也用多项金融产品专利来武装自己，即使他们在法庭案例中也反对这种做法。

sentences.id: The Bilski case involves a claimed patent on a method for hedging risk in the energy market. (17 words)
sentences.en: 比尔斯基案涉及到一份关于能源市场风险规避模式的已认证的专利。
sentences.cn: The Federal Circuit issued an unusual order stating that the case would be heard by all 12 of the court’s judges, rather than a typical panel of three, and that one issue it wants to evaluate is whether it should “reconsider” its State Street Bank ruling. (46 words)
sentences.words: 联邦巡回法庭发布了一项罕见的法令，法令宣布该案件将由全部十二名法官共同听审，而不是惯常的三人听审团进行；法令还宣布，法庭希望评估是否应该“重审”道富银行的裁决。

sentences.id: The Federal Circuit’s action comes in the wake of a series of recent decisions by the Supreme Court that has narrowed the scope of protections for patent holders. (28 words)
sentences.en: 联邦巡回法院之所以有上述行动，是因为高法院最近做出了一系列的判决，缩小了专利持有人的受保范围。
sentences.cn: Last April, for example, the justices signaled that too many patents were being upheld for “inventions” that are obvious. (19 words)
sentences.words: 例如，去年四月，法官们表示：太多的专利曾一直得到支持，授予给现在看来是显而易见的所谓的“发明”。

sentences.id: The judges on the Federal Circuit are “reacting to the anti-patent trend at the Supreme Court,” says Harold C. Wegner, a patent attorney and professor at George Washington University Law School. (31 words)
sentences.en: 乔治华盛顿大学法学院的专利法律师哈罗德·C·韦格纳教授说：“联邦巡回法院的法官正在“对最高法院的反专利态势做出反应”。
sentences.cn:
1003
In his book The Tipping Point, Malcolm Gladwell argues that “social epidemics” are driven in large part by the actions of a tiny minority of special individuals, often called influentials, who are unusually informed, persuasive, or well connected. (38 words)
sentences.words: 马尔科姆·格拉德威尔在其《引爆点》一书中指出，“社会流行潮”在很大程度上是由极少数特殊个体人群的行为推动的，就是人们常说的有影响力的人，他们都异乎寻常的博闻多识，能言善辩，人脉广泛。

sentences.id: The idea is intuitively compelling, but it doesn’t explain how ideas actually spread. (13 words)
sentences.en: 从直觉上看，马尔科姆·格拉德威尔的理论似乎很有说服力，但是并不能解释流行观念的实际传播过程。
sentences.cn: The supposed importance of influentials derives from a plausible-sounding but largely untested theory called the “two-step flow of communication”: Information flows from the media to the influentials and from them to everyone else. (33 words)
sentences.words: 所谓有影响力的人的重要性根源于一个听起来合理但多半未经证实的理论，名为“两级传播理论”，即：信息先从媒体流向有影响力的人，然后再从他们流向其他人。

sentences.id: Marketers have embraced the two-step flow because it suggests that if they can just find and influence the influentials, those select people will do most of the work for them. (30 words)
sentences.en: 营销人员已然接受这种两级传播论，因为该理论表明，若他们能够找到这些有影响力的人，并对其施加影响，这些精挑细选的人就会替他们完成大部分工作。
sentences.cn: The theory also seems to explain the sudden and unexpected popularity of certain looks, brands, or neighborhoods. (17 words)
sentences.words: 该理论似乎还解释了某些样式、品牌或地段为何会突然出乎意料地受到追捧。

sentences.id: In many such cases, a cursory search for causes finds that some small group of people was wearing, promoting, or developing whatever it is before anyone else paid attention. (29 words)
sentences.en: 对于许多诸如此类的情况，究其原因，粗略地调查发现，总是有一小群人开风气之先，不管是啥，其他人还没有注意到，他们都会率先利用，穿着打扮，推广开发这些东西。
sentences.cn: Anecdotal evidence of this kind fits nicely with the idea that only certain special people can drive trends. (18 words)
sentences.words: 这种传闻的证据正好与“只有某些特别的人才能推动潮流”这种观点不谋而合。

sentences.id: In their recent work, however, some researchers have come up with the finding that influentials have far less impact on social epidemics than is generally supposed. (26 words)
sentences.en: 但是在最近的研究中，一些研究人员发现，有影响力的人对社会流行潮流的影响力远非人们通常想象的那么大。
sentences.cn: In fact, they don’t seem to be required at all. (10 words)
sentences.words: 事实上，他们似乎根本就是无关紧要。

sentences.id: The researchers’ argument stems from a simple observation about social influence: With the exception of a few celebrities like Oprah Winfrey—whose outsize presence is primarily a function of media, not interpersonal, influence—even the most influential members of a population simply don’t interact with that many others. (48 words)
sentences.en: 研究人员的观点源于对社会影响力的简单观察：即使是一群人中最有影响力的人也根本无法与那么多的其他人互动，少数像奥普拉·温弗瑞这样的名人除外，她的非凡魅力主要是媒体影响的作用，而非人际影响。
sentences.cn: Yet it is precisely these non-celebrity influentials who, according to the two-step-flow theory, are supposed to drive social epidemics, by influencing their friends and colleagues directly. (26 words)
sentences.words: 然而，根据两级传播理论，恰好应该是这些不是名人但具有影响力的人直接影响了他们的朋友和同事，从而推动了社会流行潮。

sentences.id: For a social epidemic to occur, however, each person so affected must then influence his or her own acquaintances, who must in turn influence theirs, and so on; and just how many others pay attention to each of these people has little to do with the initial influential. (48 words)
sentences.en: 但是，一种社会流行潮要最终形成，每个受到影响的人还必须影响他自己的熟人，而后者又得相应地影响其他熟人，依此类推；但是会有多少人去关注这些熟人中的每个人，与最初有影响力的人几乎没有关系。
sentences.cn: If people in the network just two degrees removed from the initial influential prove resistant, for example, the cascade of change won’t propagate very far or affect many people. (29 words)
sentences.words: 例如，如果在这个人际网中有人与最初具有影响力的人隔了两层关系，他们最终抵制这种影响，那么这种影响的连锁反应就不会继续扩大，或者影响不到很多人。

sentences.id: Building on the basic truth about interpersonal influence, the researchers studied the dynamics of social influence by conducting thousands of computer simulations of populations, manipulating a number of variables relating to people’s ability to influence others and their tendency to be influenced. (42 words)
sentences.en: 基于人际影响力的基本事实，通过对不同人群进行了成千上万次计算机模拟，研究人员研究了社会影响的动力机制，他们控制了诸多变量，涉及影响他人的能力以及受他人影响的程度。
sentences.cn: They found that the principal requirement for what is called “global cascades”—the widespread propagation of influence through networks—is the presence not of a few influentials but, rather, of a critical mass of easily influenced people. (37 words)
sentences.words: 他们发现，影响力通过人际网络广泛传播，即所谓的“全面连锁反应”，发生的首要条件，并不是需要存在少数有影响力的人，相反，关键是需要存在大量易受影响的人群。

sentences.id:
1004
Bankers have been blaming themselves for their troubles in public. (10 words)
sentences.en: 在公开场合，银行家一直将其麻烦归咎于自身。
sentences.cn: Behind the scenes, they have been taking aim at someone else: the accounting standard-setters. (14 words)
sentences.words: 然而私底下，他们却一直把矛头对准他人：会计准则制定者。

sentences.id: Their rules, moan the banks, have forced them to report enormous losses, and it’s just not fair. (17 words)
sentences.en: 这些人定的规则，银行业抱怨说，迫使他们报告庞大损失，这不公平。
sentences.cn: These rules say they must value some assets at the price a third party would pay, not the price managers and regulators would like them to fetch. (27 words)
sentences.words: 这些规则要求他们必须以第三方愿意买入的价格，而非按照管理者和监管者期望能卖得的价格来评估资产。

sentences.id: Unfortunately, banks’ lobbying now seems to be working. (8 words)
sentences.en: 不幸的是，银行的游说现在看来似乎是有效的。
sentences.cn: The details may be unknowable, but the independence of standard-setters, essential to the proper functioning of capital markets, is being compromised. (21 words)
sentences.words: 其中细节可能无法获知，但资本市场正常运行的关键，准则制定者的独立性，正在受到损害。

sentences.id: And, unless banks carry toxic assets at prices that attract buyers, reviving the banking system will be difficult. (18 words)
sentences.en: 而且，除非银行能够将有毒资产以吸引买家的价格卖出，否则，银行系统的复苏将会非常困难。
sentences.cn: After a bruising encounter with Congress, America’s Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) rushed through rule changes. (16 words)
sentences.words: 在与国会一场激烈交锋之后，美国财务会计准则委员会（FASB）匆匆通过了准则的修改。

sentences.id: These gave banks more freedom to use models to value illiquid assets and more flexibility in recognizing losses on long-term assets in their income statements. (25 words)
sentences.en: 这些修改使得银行在使用模型评估非流动资产方面用有更大的自由，同时使得它们确认收益表中长期资产损失时更为灵活。
sentences.cn: Bob Herz, the FASB’s chairman, cried out against those who “question our motives.” (13 words)
sentences.words: 美国财会委员会主席鲍伯·赫兹对那些“怀疑我们动机”的人表示出强烈的不满。

sentences.id: Yet bank shares rose and the changes enhance what one lobbying group politely calls “the use of judgment by management.” (20 words)
sentences.en: 然而银行股票上涨了，并且这些修改加强了某个游说团队客气地谓之为“管理层的理性判断”的能力。
sentences.cn: European ministers instantly demanded that the International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) do likewise. (13 words)
sentences.words: 欧洲各国的部长旋即就要求国际会计准则委员会（IASB）也这么做。

sentences.id: The IASB says it does not want to act without overall planning, but the pressure to fold when it completes its reconstruction of rules later this year is strong. (29 words)
sentences.en: 国际会计准则委员会表示不想没有完整计划就冒然行动，但在今年晚些时候完成规则修订时，要做出妥协的压力十分巨大。
sentences.cn: Charlie McCreevy, a European commissioner, warned the IASB that it did “not live in a political vacuum” but “in the real world” and that Europe could yet develop different rules. (30 words)
sentences.words: 欧洲专员查理·麦克里维告诫国际会计准则委员会说，它“不是在政治真空中”，而是“在现实世界里”，而且欧洲总有一天会制定出不同的会计规则。

sentences.id: It was banks that were on the wrong planet, with accounts that vastly overvalued assets. (15 words)
sentences.en: 正是这些银行呆错了星球，它们的账目上的资产估值过高。
sentences.cn: Today they argue that market prices overstate losses, because they largely reflect the temporary illiquidity of markets, not the likely extent of bad debts. (24 words)
sentences.words: 如今，银行辩称，市场价格夸大了损失，因为市场价格主要反映了市场暂时的流动性不足，而非坏账可能达到的程度。

sentences.id: The truth will not be known for years. (8 words)
sentences.en: 真相要多年以后才能知道。
sentences.cn: But banks’ shares trade below their book value, suggesting that investors are skeptical. (13 words)
sentences.words: 但是，银行股票交易价格低于账面价值，这表明投资者持怀疑态度。

sentences.id: And dead markets partly reflect the paralysis of banks which will not sell assets for fear of booking losses, yet are reluctant to buy all those supposed bargains. (28 words)
sentences.en: 死寂的市场在一定程度上反应了银行的瘫痪，因为害怕账面损失不愿出售资产，也不愿意去购买那些看似不错的廉价资产。
sentences.cn: To get the system working again, losses must be recognized and dealt with. (13 words)
sentences.words: 要让银行系统重新运转起来，必须确认亏损，处理亏损。

sentences.id: America’s new plan to buy up toxic assets will not work unless banks mark assets to levels which buyers find attractive. (21 words)
sentences.en: 美国收购有毒资产的新计划只有在银行将资产定价在足够吸引买家的水平上才会有效。
sentences.cn: Successful markets require independent and even combative standard-setters. (8 words)
sentences.words: 成功的市场需要独立的，甚至是争强好胜的准则制定者。

sentences.id: The FASB and IASB have been exactly that, cleaning up rules on stock options and pensions, for example, against hostility from special interests. (23 words)
sentences.en: 美国会计准则委员会和国际会计准则委员会过去一直正是如此，例如，不顾特殊利益集团的反对，整顿股票期权和养老基金的会计准则。
sentences.cn: But by giving in to critics now they are inviting pressure to make more concessions. (15 words)
sentences.words: 但是现在向批评者妥协是自讨苦吃，他们得做出更多的让步。

sentences.id:
1101
The decision of the New York Philharmonic to hire Alan Gilbert as its next music director has been the talk of the classical-music world ever since the sudden announcement of his appointment in 2009. (34 words)
sentences.en: 2009年，纽约爱乐乐团突然宣布，决定聘请艾伦·吉尔伯特为下任音乐总监，从那之后，这一决定就成了古典音乐界谈论的话题。
sentences.cn: For the most part, the response has been favorable, to say the least. (13 words)
sentences.words: 反应大体上是积极的，至少可以这样说。

sentences.id: “Hooray! (1 words)
sentences.en: “好啊！
sentences.cn: At last!” wrote Anthony Tommasini, a sober-sided classical-music critic. (9 words)
sentences.words: 终于！”连不苟言笑的古典音乐评论家安东尼·托马西尼也这样写到。

sentences.id: One of the reasons why the appointment came as such a surprise, however, is that Gilbert is comparatively little known. (20 words)
sentences.en: 但是，该任命显得很唐突的原因之一在于吉尔伯特不太有名。
sentences.cn: Even Tommasini, who had advocated Gilbert’s appointment in the Times, calls him “an unpretentious musician with no air of the formidable conductor about him.” (24 words)
sentences.words: 甚至连曾在《泰晤士报》上撰文支持任命吉尔伯特的托马斯尼都说他是“一个低调的音乐家，没有指挥家那种大架子”。

sentences.id: As a description of the next music director of an orchestra that has hitherto been led by musicians like Gustav Mahler and Pierre Boulez, that seems likely to have struck at least some Times readers as faint praise. (38 words)
sentences.en: 对一个迄今为止一直是由古斯塔夫·马勒和皮埃尔·布列兹这样的音乐家来率领的交响乐团，这样描述其下一任指挥，似乎至少会让一些《时报》的读者觉得赞扬来得有点敷衍。
sentences.cn: For my part, I have no idea whether Gilbert is a great conductor or even a good one. (18 words)
sentences.words: 对我来说，我不知道吉尔伯特是不是一个伟大的指挥家；也不知道他到底算不算是一个好指挥。

sentences.id: To be sure, he performs an impressive variety of interesting compositions, but it is not necessary for me to visit Avery Fisher Hall, or anywhere else, to hear interesting orchestral music. (31 words)
sentences.en: 肯定，他指挥过许多有意义的作品，令人印象深刻；但要听有意义的交响乐，我没有必要非要去艾弗里·费雪厅，或别的什么地方。
sentences.cn: All I have to do is to go to my CD shelf, or boot up my computer and download still more recorded music from iTunes. (25 words)
sentences.words: 我只需要走到唱片架播放唱片就行，还可以打开电脑从 itunes 下载更多录制音乐。

sentences.id: Devoted concertgoers who reply that recordings are no substitute for live performance are missing the point. (16 words)
sentences.en: 那些常去音乐会的忠实听众会回答说录音和听现场没得比，他们没有抓住问题的核心。
sentences.cn: For the time, attention, and money of the art-loving public, classical instrumentalists must compete not only with opera houses, dance troupes, theater companies, and museums, but also with the recorded performances of the great classical musicians of the 20th century. (40 words)
sentences.words: 为了争取艺术爱好者投入时间、精力和财力，古典音乐演奏家不仅必须与歌剧院、舞蹈团、话剧团、博物馆竞争，而且还必须与20世纪的伟大古典音乐家的演奏唱片竞争。

sentences.id: These recordings are cheap, available everywhere, and very often much higher in artistic quality than today’s live performances; moreover, they can be “consumed” at a time and place of the listener’s choosing. (32 words)
sentences.en: 这些唱片可谓是物美价廉，随处可得，艺术品质比起如今的现场演奏要高很多；再说，听众可以选择时间和地方“消费”这些音乐。
sentences.cn: The widespread availability of such recordings has thus brought about a crisis in the institution of the traditional classical concert. (20 words)
sentences.words: 这样的唱片唾手可得、四处泛滥，因此使得传统古典音乐演奏会的习俗陷入危机。

sentences.id: One possible response is for classical performers to program attractive new music that is not yet available on record. (19 words)
sentences.en: 对于古典音乐演奏家而言，有一种可能的对策，就是可以去演奏尚未录制成唱片的，吸引人的新音乐作品。
sentences.cn: Gilbert’s own interest in new music has been widely noted: Alex Ross, a classical-music critic, has described him as a man who is capable of turning the Philharmonic into “a markedly different, more vibrant organization.” (35 words)
sentences.words: 吉尔伯特本人在新音乐方面的兴趣，大家有目共睹：古典音乐评论家亚历克斯·罗斯就说他有能力把纽约爱乐乐团变成“一个截然不同，更有生机的团体”。

sentences.id: But what will be the nature of that difference? (9 words)
sentences.en: 但这会有什么本质上的不同吗？
sentences.cn: Merely expanding the orchestra’s repertoire will not be enough. (9 words)
sentences.words: 仅仅靠扩大乐团的节目单是不够的。

sentences.id: If Gilbert and the Philharmonic are to succeed, they must first change the relationship between America’s oldest orchestra and the new audience it hopes to attract. (26 words)
sentences.en: 如果吉尔伯特和纽约爱乐想成功的话，他们就必须首先要改变美国历史最悠久的交响乐团及其希望吸引的新听众之间的关系。
sentences.cn:
1102
When Liam McGee departed as president of Bank of America in August, his explanation was surprisingly straight up. (18 words)
sentences.words: 八月，利亚姆·麦吉辞去美国银行总裁一职时，他的解释之直白，令人惊讶。

sentences.id: Rather than cloaking his exit in the usual vague excuses, he came right out and said he was leaving “to pursue my goal of running a company.” (27 words)
sentences.en: 他没有用通常模糊的借口来掩盖自己的退出，而是直截了当地说，他离开“是要追求自己目标，开一家公司”。
sentences.cn: Broadcasting his ambition was “very much my decision,” McGee says. (10 words)
sentences.words: 公开宣布志向“就是我自己的决定”，麦吉说。

sentences.id: Within two weeks, he was talking for the first time with the board of Hartford Financial Services Group, which named him CEO and chairman on September 29. (27 words)
sentences.en: 辞职不到两周，他就与美国哈特福德金融服务公司董事会开始第一次面谈。9月29日，该公司任命他为董事长兼首席执行官。
sentences.cn: McGee says leaving without a position lined up gave him time to reflect on what kind of company he wanted to run. (22 words)
sentences.words: 麦吉说没有找到下家就辞职让他有时间去思考他想去经营什么样的公司。

sentences.id: It also sent a clear message to the outside world about his aspirations. (13 words)
sentences.en: 这也给外界发出了一个明确的信号，表明他的志向。
sentences.cn: And McGee isn’t alone. (4 words)
sentences.words: 并且这样做的，不仅仅只有麦吉一个人。

sentences.id: In recent weeks the No. 2 executives at Avon and American Express quit with the explanation that they were looking for a CEO post. (24 words)
sentences.en: 最近几周，雅芳和美国运通两家公司的二号高管分别离职，他们解释道他们一直在寻求首席执行官职位。
sentences.cn: As boards scrutinize succession plans in response to shareholder pressure, executives who don’t get the nod also may wish to move on. (22 words)
sentences.words: 董事会迫于股东压力仔细审查续任计划，没能得到点头认可的高管也可能想跳槽。

sentences.id: A turbulent business environment also has senior managers cautious of letting vague pronouncements cloud their reputations. (16 words)
sentences.en: 同时，动荡的商业环境也会让高级经理人小心翼翼，以避免模糊不清的表态让自己的声誉蒙上阴影。
sentences.cn: As the first signs of recovery begin to take hold, deputy chiefs may be more willing to make the jump without a net. (23 words)
sentences.words: 随着经济复苏的势头开始变得越来越强劲，副总们即使没有找到下家，也更敢大胆跳槽。

sentences.id: In the third quarter, CEO turnover was down 23% from a year ago as nervous boards stuck with the leaders they had, according to Liberum Research. (26 words)
sentences.en: 利伯伦研究公司的数据表明，由于忧心忡忡的董事会都保留了现有的管理层，所以第三季度首席执行官的人事变动率比去年下降了23%。
sentences.cn: As the economy picks up, opportunities will abound for aspiring leaders. (11 words)
sentences.words: 随着经济好转，对一些胸怀壮志的领导者来说，到处都是机遇。

sentences.id: The decision to quit a senior position to look for a better one is unconventional. (15 words)
sentences.en: 决定要辞去高级职位，去寻找一份更好的，并不寻常。
sentences.cn: For years executives and headhunters have adhered to the rule that the most attractive CEO candidates are the ones who must be poached. (23 words)
sentences.words: 多年来，公司管理者和猎头都秉持这样的原则：最有吸引力的首席执行官人选是那种必须要挖过来的人。

sentences.id: Says Korn/Ferry senior partner Dennis Carey: “I can’t think of a single search I’ve done where a board has not instructed me to look at sitting CEOs first.” (28 words)
sentences.en: 光辉国际的大股东丹尼斯•凯利说：“我能想到的是，我曾做过的每一次招聘，董事会都会要求我首先关注那些在任的首席执行官。”
sentences.cn: Those who jumped without a job haven’t always landed in top positions quickly. (13 words)
sentences.words: 那些没有找到工作就跳的并不总是马上就能在高位着陆。

sentences.id: Ellen Marram quit as chief of Tropicana a decade ago, saying she wanted to be a CEO. (17 words)
sentences.en: 十年前，埃伦·马拉姆辞去了纯果乐公司负责人职务，说自己想做一名首席执行官。
sentences.cn: It was a year before she became head of a tiny Internet-based commodities exchange. (14 words)
sentences.words: 一年以后，她才成了一家小型网上商品交易公司的领导。

sentences.id: Robert Willumstad left Citigroup in 2005 with ambitions to be a CEO. (12 words)
sentences.en: 2005年，罗伯特·维伦斯坦德离开花旗集团，雄心勃勃地想做一名首席执行官。
sentences.cn: He finally took that post at a major financial institution three years later. (13 words)
sentences.words: 但三年后，他才终于在一家大型金融机构如愿以偿。

sentences.id: Many recruiters say the old disgrace is fading for top performers. (11 words)
sentences.en: 许多搞招聘的说，对于出类拔萃的人才来说，老一套那种离职不光彩的感觉，逐渐淡化。
sentences.cn: The financial crisis has made it more acceptable to be between jobs or to leave a bad one. (18 words)
sentences.words: 待业，或者辞掉一份烂工作，金融危机下，大家都变得更容易接受。

sentences.id: “The traditional rule was it’s safer to stay where you are, but that’s been fundamentally inverted,” says one headhunter. (19 words)
sentences.en: “传统的做法是最好不要动窝，但现在已经彻底反过来了，”一个猎头如是说。
sentences.cn: “The people who’ve been hurt the worst are those who’ve stayed too long.” (13 words)
sentences.words: 呆得时间越长越受伤。

sentences.id:
1103
The rough guide to marketing success used to be that you got what you paid for. (16 words)
sentences.en: 过去市场营销成功的大致方针是：付出多少，得到多少。
sentences.cn: No longer. (2 words)
sentences.words: 然而，今非昔比了。

sentences.id: While traditional “paid” media—such as television commercials and print advertisements—still play a major role, companies today can exploit many alternative forms of media. (25 words)
sentences.en: 尽管传统的“付费”媒体，比如电视广告和平面广告，作用依然很大；但如今企业还可以利用许多其他形式的媒体。
sentences.cn: Consumers passionate about a product may create “earned” media by willingly promoting it to friends, and a company may leverage “owned” media by sending e-mail alerts about products and sales to customers registered with its Web site. (37 words)
sentences.words: 例如，酷爱某种产品的消费者可能会乐意地向朋友推荐该产品，从而创建 “口碑”媒体；而一家企业可以充分利用“自有”媒体，向在其网站上注册过的顾客发送电子邮件，提醒他们关注公司产品和促销信息。

sentences.id: In fact, the way consumers now approach the process of making purchase decisions means that marketing’s impact stems from a broad range of factors beyond conventional paid media. (28 words)
sentences.en: 事实上，现在消费者做出购买决定这一过程的处理方式意味着，市场营销的影响力除了来自传统付费媒体，还有其他诸多因素。
sentences.cn: Paid and owned media are controlled by marketers promoting their own products. (12 words)
sentences.words: 营销商可以控制付费媒体和自有媒体来推销自己的产品。

sentences.id: For earned media, such marketers act as the initiator for users’ responses. (12 words)
sentences.en: 对于口碑媒体，这些营销商就是激发用户反应的牵头人。
sentences.cn: But in some cases, one marketer’s owned media become another marketer’s paid media—for instance, when an e-commerce retailer sells ad space on its Web site. (26 words)
sentences.words: 但在某些情况下，例如，如果某个电子商务零售商出售其网站上的广告位置，那一个营销商的自有媒体就会成为另一个营销商的付费媒体。

sentences.id: We define such sold media as owned media whose traffic is so strong that other organizations place their content or e-commerce engines within that environment. (25 words)
sentences.en: 我们将这种出售媒体定义为自有媒体：其流量之大，别的机构也会来投放内容或电子商务引擎。
sentences.cn: This trend, which we believe is still in its infancy, effectively began with retailers and travel providers such as airlines and hotels and will no doubt go further. (28 words)
sentences.words: 这种趋势，我们认为目前还在初期，它实际上肇始于零售商以及航空公司和酒店等旅游供应商，无疑，它将会走得更远。

sentences.id: Johnson & Johnson, for example, has created BabyCenter, a stand-alone media property that promotes complementary and even competitive products. (19 words)
sentences.en: 强生公司，比如，它创建了宝宝中心网（BabyCenter），一家独立的媒体平台，用于推广一些具有互补性甚至竞争性的产品。
sentences.cn: Besides generating income, the presence of other marketers makes the site seem objective, gives companies opportunities to learn valuable information about the appeal of other companies’ marketing, and may help expand user traffic for all companies concerned. (37 words)
sentences.words: 其他营销商的存在，除了能带来收入，还使网站显得比较客观，使一些企业有机会获得宝贵的信息，了解到其他公司营销活动的独到之处，而且，或许还有助于增加所有相关企业的用户流量。

sentences.id: The same dramatic technological changes that have provided marketers with more (and more diverse) communications choices have also increased the risk that passionate consumers will voice their opinions in quicker, more visible, and much more damaging ways. (37 words)
sentences.en: 同样剧烈的技术变革使营销人员在沟通方式上有了更多的（且更多样化的）选择，也带来了更大的风险：因为情绪激动的消费者表达意见的方式也会更快、更明显和更具破坏性。
sentences.cn: Such hijacked media are the opposite of earned media: an asset or campaign becomes hostage to consumers, other stakeholders, or activists who make negative allegations about a brand or product. (30 words)
sentences.words: 这种劫持媒体与口碑媒体正好相反：某一种资产或者某个商业活动会变成消费者、其他利益相关者，或者积极分子的人质，对某一个品牌或者产品发布负面评论。

sentences.id: Members of social networks, for instance, are learning that they can hijack media to apply pressure on the businesses that originally created them. (23 words)
sentences.en: 社交网络的用户，比如，他们正在认识到可以劫持媒体向最初创建这些媒体的企业施压。
sentences.cn: If that happens, passionate consumers would try to persuade others to boycott products, putting the reputation of the target company at risk. (22 words)
sentences.words: 若此类事情发生，情绪激动的消费者会设法劝说他人抵制产品，从而危及目标企业的声誉。

sentences.id: In such a case, the company’s response may not be sufficiently quick or thoughtful, and the learning curve has been steep. (21 words)
sentences.en: 在这种情况下，公司的反应或许不够快，也或许不够周全；但是要尽快了解事态发展。
sentences.cn: Toyota Motor, for example, alleviated some of the damage from its recall crisis earlier this year with a relatively quick and well-orchestrated social-media response campaign, which included efforts to engage with consumers directly on sites such as Twitter and the social-news site Digg. (43 words)
sentences.words: 例如，丰田汽车在今年年初的召回危机中，采用相对较快且安排得当的社交媒体回应活动，包括在推特（Twitter）和社会新闻网站掘客（Digg）等网站上尽量直接与消费者交流，从而减少了损失。

sentences.id:
1104
It’s no surprise that Jennifer Senior’s insightful, provocative magazine cover story, “I Love My Children, I Hate My Life,” is arousing much chatter—nothing gets people talking like the suggestion that child rearing is anything less than a completely fulfilling, life-enriching experience. (42 words)
sentences.en: 詹妮弗·西尼尔为一本杂志撰写的封面故事《我爱娃，但我讨厌生活》，见解独到，发人深省，文章引起议论纷纷，不足为奇。文章中提到养孩子根本不是一件令人完全满意、丰富人生的事情，没有什么比这一观点更能激发人们的谈兴了。
sentences.cn: Rather than concluding that children make parents either happy or miserable, Senior suggests we need to redefine happiness: instead of thinking of it as something that can be measured by moment-to-moment joy, we should consider being happy as a past-tense condition. (41 words)
sentences.words: 西尼尔并没有对养孩子让父母幸福还是痛苦这个问题做出结论，但建议我们需重新定义幸福：幸福不是可以用时时开心来衡量的东西，我们应该把幸福视为过去时的状态。

sentences.id: Even though the day-to-day experience of raising kids can be soul-crushingly hard, Senior writes that “the very things that in the moment dampen our moods can later be sources of intense gratification and delight.” (34 words)
sentences.en: 尽管抚养孩子日复一日的生活，令人心力交瘁，十分艰难，西尼尔写道，但“正是那些一时让我们情绪低落的事情带来了日后由衷的欣喜。”
sentences.cn: The magazine cover showing an attractive mother holding a cute baby is hardly the only Madonna-and-child image on newsstands this week. (21 words)
sentences.words: 杂志封面上有一个漂亮的妈妈抱着一个可爱的宝宝，很难说这是本周报摊上唯一的“圣母子”形象。

sentences.id: There are also stories about newly adoptive—and newly single—mom Sandra Bullock, as well as the usual “Jennifer Aniston is pregnant” news. (23 words)
sentences.en: 还有些故事，说的是桑德拉·布洛克刚收养了孩子成了单亲妈妈，也有“詹妮弗·安妮斯顿怀孕了”这一类惯常的新闻。
sentences.cn: Practically every week features at least one celebrity mom, or mom-to-be, smiling on the newsstands. (15 words)
sentences.words: 实际上，他们每周都会重点推出至少一位明星妈妈或者准妈妈，报摊上，笑容可掬。

sentences.id: In a society that so persistently celebrates procreation, is it any wonder that admitting you regret having children is equivalent to admitting you support kitten-killing? (25 words)
sentences.en: 在一个社会中，都执着于对生育歌功颂德，生了娃，承认自己感到后悔就等于承认自己赞成虐杀小猫，这有什么奇怪的吗？
sentences.cn: It doesn’t seem quite fair, then, to compare the regrets of parents to the regrets of the childless. (18 words)
sentences.words: 这样看来，将“为人父母的后悔”和“膝下无子的遗憾”相提并论，似乎有失公允。

sentences.id: Unhappy parents rarely are provoked to wonder if they shouldn’t have had kids, but unhappy childless folks are bothered with the message that children are the single most important thing in the world: obviously their misery must be a direct result of the gaping baby-size holes in their lives. (49 words)
sentences.en: 几乎没有什么事情会促使那些感觉不快乐的父母去思考是否他们本来就不应该要孩子，但是那些没有孩子也感觉不快乐的人也会感到困扰，因为有人说在这个世界上孩子才是唯一最重要的：显然，他们之所以痛苦肯定是因为生活中没有孩子而带来的空洞。
sentences.cn: Of course, the image of parenthood that celebrity magazines like Us Weekly and People present is hugely unrealistic, especially when the parents are single mothers like Bullock. (27 words)
sentences.words: 当然，《我们周刊》和《人物》等名人杂志上刊登出来的父母形象是非常不切实际的，尤其是当父母还是像布洛克这样的单亲妈妈时更是如此。

sentences.id: According to several studies concluding that parents are less happy than childless couples, single parents are the least happy of all. (21 words)
sentences.en: 有多项研究得出结论：有孩子的不如没有孩子的幸福，据此，单亲父母最不幸福。
sentences.cn: No shock there, considering how much work it is to raise a kid without a partner to lean on; yet to hear Sandra and Britney tell it, raising a kid on their “own” (read: with round-the-clock help) is a piece of cake. (42 words)
sentences.words: 不足为奇，想想看，一个人拉扯一个娃长大，没有人可依靠，得多费事。然而，你听听桑德拉和布兰妮怎么说的：“独自”（解读：全天候都有人帮忙）带娃，小菜一碟。

sentences.id: It’s hard to imagine that many people are dumb enough to want children just because Reese and Angelina make it look so glamorous: most adults understand that a baby is not a haircut. (33 words)
sentences.en: 很难想象，许多人蠢到想要孩子，仅仅是因为里斯和安吉丽娜使其看起来如此光鲜亮丽：大多数成年人都明白，养娃不是理发。
sentences.cn: But it’s interesting to wonder if the images we see every week of stress-free, happiness-enhancing parenthood aren’t in some small, subconscious way contributing to our own dissatisfactions with the actual experience, in the same way that a small part of us hoped getting “the Rachel” might make us look just a little bit like Jennifer Aniston. (56 words)
sentences.words: 但是，我们每周看到的那些无忧无虑、幸福满满的父母形象是否会以某种难以察觉的、无意识的方式减轻我们自己对为人父母现实体验的不满呢？正如我们少数人曾经希望自己剪个“瑞秋发型”，好让自己看起来有点像詹妮弗·安妮斯顿，想想这些，有点意思。

sentences.id:
1201
Come on—Everybody’s doing it. (5 words)
sentences.en: 快点吧,大家都在这么做呢。
sentences.cn: That whispered message, half invitation and half forcing, is what most of us think of when we hear the words peer pressure. (22 words)
sentences.words: 这种耳边听到话，半邀请，半强迫，当我们听到“同辈压力”这个词的时候，想到的就是这个话。

sentences.id: It usually leads to no good— drinking, drugs and casual sex. (11 words)
sentences.en: 通常，它带来的绝非好事，比如喝酒、吸毒、滥交。
sentences.cn: But in her new book Join the Club, Tina Rosenberg contends that peer pressure can also be a positive force through what she calls the social cure, in which organizations and officials use the power of group dynamics to help individuals improve their lives and possibly the world. (48 words)
sentences.words: 但蒂娜·罗森堡在其新著《加入俱乐部》中主张，通过她所说的社会治疗，同辈压力也可以成为一种积极的力量。在社会治疗中，各机构和官员可以使用群体力量来帮助个人改善生活，甚至可能改造世界。

sentences.id: Rosenberg, the recipient of a Pulitzer Prize, offers a host of examples of the social cure in action: In South Carolina, a state-sponsored antismoking program called Rage Against the Haze sets out to make cigarettes uncool. (36 words)
sentences.en: 罗森堡是普利策奖获得者，她提供了许多社会治疗的例子：在南卡罗莱纳州，有一个州政府资助的反吸烟的项目，名为“怒怼烟雾”，就致力于让吸烟成老土。
sentences.cn: In South Africa, an HIV-prevention initiative known as loveLife recruits young people to promote safe sex among their peers. (19 words)
sentences.words: 在南非，一场预防艾滋病病毒的活动，名为“热爱生命”，正在招募年轻人让他们在同龄人中宣传安全性行为。

sentences.id: The idea seems promising, and Rosenberg is a perceptive observer. (10 words)
sentences.en: 这个想法似乎大有可为，并且罗森堡的确是个敏锐的观察家。
sentences.cn: Her critique of the lameness of many public-health campaigns is spot-on: they fail to mobilize peer pressure for healthy habits, and they demonstrate a seriously flawed understanding of psychology. (29 words)
sentences.words: 她对于许多公共健康活动不足之处的批评是中肯的：这些活动没能利用同辈压力来建立起健康的习惯，并表现出对心理学存在严重的误解。

sentences.id: “Dare to be different, please don’t smoke!” pleads one billboard campaign aimed at reducing smoking among teenagers—teenagers, who desire nothing more than fitting in. (25 words)
sentences.en: “敢做不同，敢不抽烟”，一个广告牌宣传活动这样呼吁道，活动旨在青少年——最渴望融入的青少年中减少吸烟。
sentences.cn: Rosenberg argues convincingly that public-health advocates ought to take a page from advertisers, so skilled at applying peer pressure. (19 words)
sentences.words: 罗森堡提出了一个观点令人信服，她认为：公共健康倡导者应该向善于应用同辈压力的广告商学习。

sentences.id: But on the general effectiveness of the social cure, Rosenberg is less persuasive. (13 words)
sentences.en: 但若论社会治疗的整体效果，罗森堡就没有那么有说服力了。
sentences.cn: Join the Club is filled with too much irrelevant detail and not enough exploration of the social and biological factors that make peer pressure so powerful. (26 words)
sentences.words: 《加入俱乐部》一书，充斥着太多无关细节，却没有充分探讨那些使同辈压力变得如此强大的社会和生物因素。

sentences.id: The most glaring flaw of the social cure as it’s presented here is that it doesn’t work very well for very long. (22 words)
sentences.en: 正如这里指出，社会治疗的明显缺陷是其不能长期发挥良好的效果。
sentences.cn: Rage Against the Haze failed once state funding was cut. (10 words)
sentences.words: “怒怼烟雾”项目在州政府停止自助后失败了。

sentences.id: Evidence that the loveLife program produces lasting changes is limited and mixed. (12 words)
sentences.en: 鲜有证据表明“热爱生命”的活动能产生持久的影响，且基本都是毁誉参半。
sentences.cn: There’s no doubt that our peer groups exert enormous influence on our behavior. (13 words)
sentences.words: 毫无疑问，同龄人会对我们的行为产生巨大影响。

sentences.id: An emerging body of research shows that positive health habits—as well as negative ones—spread through networks of friends via social communication. (23 words)
sentences.en: 不断涌现出的大量研究显示，无论是良好还是良的健康习惯，都会通过社会交际在朋友之间传播。
sentences.cn: This is a subtle form of peer pressure: we unconsciously imitate the behavior we see every day. (17 words)
sentences.words: 这种同辈压力很微妙：我们不知不觉地会模仿自己每天看到的行为。

sentences.id: Far less certain, however, is how successfully experts and bureaucrats can select our peer groups and steer their activities in virtuous directions. (22 words)
sentences.en:
但是，很难确定的是，专家和官员能在多大程度上成功选出我们的同龄群体，
并引导其行为良性发展。
sentences.cn: It’s like the teacher who breaks up the troublemakers in the back row by pairing them with better-behaved classmates. (19 words)
sentences.words: 这就像老师，把后排的捣蛋鬼分开，安排他们和更乖巧的同学坐一起。

sentences.id: The tactic never really works. (5 words)
sentences.en: 这种方法从来就没有什么效果。
sentences.cn: And that’s the problem with a social cure engineered from the outside: in the real world, as in school, we insist on choosing our own friends. (26 words)
sentences.words: 这就是外力促成的社会治疗所存在的问题：在现实世界，跟在学校一样，我们坚持自己选择自己的朋友。

sentences.id:
1202
A deal is a deal—except, apparently, when Entergy is involved. (11 words)
sentences.en: 协议就是协议——除非，看样子，涉及到安特吉公司。
sentences.cn: The company, a major energy supplier in New England, provoked justified outrage in Vermont last week when it announced it was reneging on a longstanding commitment to abide by the state’s strict nuclear regulations. (34 words)
sentences.words: 该公司是新英格兰地区一家主要能源提供商，因上周宣布终止履行遵守严格的核能法规的长期承诺，激起佛蒙特州人民的义愤。

sentences.id: Instead, the company has done precisely what it had long promised it would not: challenge the constitutionality of Vermont’s rules in the federal court, as part of a desperate effort to keep its Vermont Yankee nuclear power plant running. (39 words)
sentences.en: 相反，该公司恰好做了它长期以来承诺不会做的事情：在联邦法庭上质疑佛蒙特州规定是否合乎宪法，孤注一掷，力保佛蒙特扬基核电厂的运营。
sentences.cn: It’s a stunning move. (4 words)
sentences.words: 这真是惊人之举。

sentences.id: The conflict has been surfacing since 2002, when the corporation bought Vermont’s only nuclear power plant, an aging reactor in Vernon. (21 words)
sentences.en: 这场冲突自2002年就已初显端倪，当时该公司购买了佛蒙特州唯一的核电厂，一座位于佛侬镇的破旧反应堆。
sentences.cn: As a condition of receiving state approval for the sale, the company agreed to seek permission from state regulators to operate past 2012. (23 words)
sentences.words: 作为州政府许可交易的一项条件，该公司同意在2012年后的运营需征求州监管机构的批准。

sentences.id: In 2006, the state went a step further, requiring that any extension of the plant’s license be subject to Vermont legislature’s approval. (22 words)
sentences.en: 2006年，州政府又进一步要求，若要延期电厂经营许可，需获得佛蒙特立法机关的批准。
sentences.cn: Then, too, the company went along. (6 words)
sentences.words: 当时，该公司也并无异议。

sentences.id: Either Entergy never really intended to live by those commitments, or it simply didn’t foresee what would happen next. (19 words)
sentences.en: 安特吉要么从来没有真正打算遵守这些承诺，要么是根本没有预料到接下来会发生什么事情。
sentences.cn: A string of accidents, including the partial collapse of a cooling tower in 2007 and the discovery of an underground pipe system leakage, raised serious questions about both Vermont Yankee’s safety and Entergy’s management—especially after the company made misleading statements about the pipe. (44 words)
sentences.words: 一系列的事件，包括2007年一座冷却塔部分倒塌，还有一个地下管道系统被人发现泄露，使人们严重怀疑佛蒙特扬基电站的安全问题和安吉特的管理水平——尤其是在该公司对其管道泄露问题做出欺骗性的声明之后。

sentences.id: Enraged by Entergy’s behavior, the Vermont Senate voted 26 to 4 last year against allowing an extension. (17 words)
sentences.en: 在安特吉公司的行为触怒之下，佛蒙特州参议院去年以26比4的投票反对其延期申请。
sentences.cn: Now the company is suddenly claiming that the 2002 agreement is invalid because of the 2006 legislation, and that only the federal government has regulatory power over nuclear issues. (29 words)
sentences.words: 现在安吉特公司突然宣布，因与2006年的法规冲突，2002年的协定无效，并且只有联邦政府才对核问题有管理权。

sentences.id: The legal issues in the case are obscure: whereas the Supreme Court has ruled that states do have some regulatory authority over nuclear power, legal scholars say that Vermont case will offer a precedent-setting test of how far those powers extend. (41 words)
sentences.en: 此案涉及的法律问题模糊不清：尽管最高法院规定各州对核电站的确有一定的监管权，但法律专家却认为，佛蒙特案件会成为考量各州政府权限范围的先例。
sentences.cn: Certainly, there are valid concerns about the patchwork regulations that could result if every state sets its own rules. (19 words)
sentences.words: 当然，如果每个州都自己立法，确实有理由担心会由此产生各种补丁法规。

sentences.id: But had Entergy kept its word, that debate would be beside the point. (13 words)
sentences.en: 但若是安特吉公司能信守诺言，这种争议也无关紧要了。
sentences.cn: The company seems to have concluded that its reputation in Vermont is already so damaged that it has nothing left to lose by going to war with the state. (29 words)
sentences.words: 该公司似乎认定，在佛蒙特已经声名狼藉，没有什么后顾之忧，不如和州政府背水一战。

sentences.id: But there should be consequences. (5 words)
sentences.en: 但有因必有果。
sentences.cn: Permission to run a nuclear plant is a public trust. (10 words)
sentences.words: 核电厂的经营许可，是公众的一种信任。

sentences.id: Entergy runs 11 other reactors in the United States, including Pilgrim Nuclear station in Plymouth. (15 words)
sentences.en: 安特吉在美国还经营有另外11个反应堆，包括普利茅斯的朝圣者核电站。
sentences.cn: Pledging to run Pilgrim safely, the company has applied for federal permission to keep it open for another 20 years. (20 words)
sentences.words: 该公司承诺安全经营朝圣者核电站，并提出申请，要求联邦政府同意其再继续经营20年。

sentences.id: But as the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) reviews the company’s application, it should keep in mind what promises from Entergy are worth. (22 words)
sentences.en: 但是，核能管理委员会(NRC)在审查该公司申请的时候，它应该会掂量安特吉的承诺价值几何。
sentences.cn:
1203
In the idealized version of how science is done, facts about the world are waiting to be observed and collected by objective researchers who use the scientific method to carry out their work. (33 words)
sentences.words: 搞科研的理想模式是，研究人员从事实出发，使用科学的方法进行科学研究，观察、收集这个世界等待探索的真相。

sentences.id: But in the everyday practice of science, discovery frequently follows an ambiguous and complicated route. (15 words)
sentences.en: 但在日常科学实践中，科学发现的过程通常模糊而又复杂。
sentences.cn: We aim to be objective, but we cannot escape the context of our unique life experience. (16 words)
sentences.words: 我们力图做到客观公正，但我们却无法摆脱各自独特人生经历所发生的环境。

sentences.id: Prior knowledge and interests influence what we experience, what we think our experiences mean, and the subsequent actions we take. (20 words)
sentences.en: 我们已有的知识和兴趣会影响我们的经历，影响我们对自己经历的理解，也会影响后续采取的行动。
sentences.cn: Opportunities for misinterpretation, error, and self-deception abound. (7 words)
sentences.words: 到处都可能会出现误解、犯错、自欺欺人的现象。

sentences.id: Consequently, discovery claims should be thought of as protoscience. (9 words)
sentences.en: 因此，科学发现的声明应看作是科学雏形。
sentences.cn: Similar to newly staked mining claims, they are full of potential. (11 words)
sentences.words: 类似于新公布的采矿权声明，充满各种可能。

sentences.id: But it takes collective scrutiny and acceptance to transform a discovery claim into a mature discovery. (16 words)
sentences.en: 但是发现申明需要集体审查和认可才能转变为成熟的发现。
sentences.cn: This is the credibility process, through which the individual researcher’s me, here, now becomes the community’s anyone, anywhere, anytime. (19 words)
sentences.words: 这就是取信过程，通过这一过程，个体研究人员的“我、此时、此地”变成科学界的“任何人、任何地方、任何时间”。

sentences.id: Objective knowledge is the goal, not the starting point. (9 words)
sentences.en: 客观知识是目的，而非起点。
sentences.cn: Once a discovery claim becomes public, the discoverer receives intellectual credit. (11 words)
sentences.words: 发现申明一旦公开，发现者即获得知识赞誉。

sentences.id: But, unlike with mining claims, the community takes control of what happens next. (13 words)
sentences.en: 与采矿声明不同，科学界控制下一步的发展。
sentences.cn: Within the complex social structure of the scientific community, researchers make discoveries; editors and reviewers act as gatekeepers by controlling the publication process; other scientists use the new finding to suit their own purposes; and finally, the public (including other scientists) receives the new discovery and possibly accompanying technology. (49 words)
sentences.words: 在科学界复杂的社会结构中，研究人员做出发现；编辑和审校人员则是守门员，控制发表过程；其他科学家使用这一新发现达成各自的目标；最后，公众，包括其他科学家，获知这一新发现以及应运而生的新技术。

sentences.id: As a discovery claim works its way through the community, the interaction and confrontation between shared and competing beliefs about the science and the technology involved transforms an individual’s discovery claim into the community’s credible discovery. (36 words)
sentences.en: 在一项发现声明通过业界审查的过程中，人们对牵涉到的科学和技术有相同或者相左的一些观点，这些观点之间的相互影响和碰撞把个体的发现声明转变成了业界值得信赖的发现。
sentences.cn: Two paradoxes exist throughout this credibility process. (7 words)
sentences.words: 整个取信过程存在两种悖论。

sentences.id: First, scientific work tends to focus on some aspect of prevailing knowledge that is viewed as incomplete or incorrect. (19 words)
sentences.en: 其一，科学研究往往关注现有知识中大家认为是不完整或者不正确的方面。
sentences.cn: Little reward accompanies duplication and confirmation of what is already known and believed. (13 words)
sentences.words: 重复和证实人们已经知道或者相信的知识鲜有回报。

sentences.id: The goal is new-search, not re-search. (6 words)
sentences.en: 科学研究的目标是新的研究，而不是重复研究。
sentences.cn: Not surprisingly, newly published discovery claims and credible discoveries that appear to be important and convincing will always be open to challenge and potential modification or refutation by future researchers. (30 words)
sentences.words: 新发表的发现声明和可靠发现貌似至关重要，令人信服，但总是容易受到后来研究人员的质疑，可能会加以修改，甚至推翻，这不足为奇。

sentences.id: Second, novelty itself frequently provokes disbelief. (6 words)
sentences.en: 其二，新奇的事物本身就常常会招致怀疑。
sentences.cn: Nobel Laureate and physiologist Albert Szent-Györgyi once described discovery as “seeing what everybody has seen and thinking what nobody has thought.” (21 words)
sentences.words: 诺贝尔奖获得者、生理学家阿尔贝特·圣捷尔吉曾把发现描述为“见世人所见，思无人所思”。

sentences.id: But thinking what nobody else has thought and telling others what they have missed may not change their views. (19 words)
sentences.en: 但思无人所思，且告知他人疏漏之处，未必会改变其观点。
sentences.cn: Sometimes years are required for truly novel discovery claims to be accepted and appreciated. (14 words)
sentences.words: 有时候，真正新颖的发现声明需要多年才会得到接受和认可。

sentences.id: In the end, credibility “happens” to a discovery claim—a process that corresponds to what philosopher Annette Baier has described as the commons of the mind. (26 words)
sentences.en: 最后，一项发现声明“发现”是可信的——这一过程是与哲学家安妮特·拜尔描述的“思维的交汇”相符合。
sentences.cn: “We reason together, challenge, revise, and complete each other’s reasoning and each other’s conceptions of reason.” (16 words)
sentences.words: 我们一起理解、质疑、修改，进而完善彼此的推理以及彼此对理性的看法。

sentences.id:
1204
If the trade unionist Jimmy Hoffa were alive today, he would probably represent civil servants. (15 words)
sentences.en: 如果工会主义者吉米·霍夫尔现在还在世，他可能会代表公务员。
sentences.cn: When Hoffa’s Teamsters were in their prime in 1960, only one in ten American government workers belonged to a union; now 36% do. (23 words)
sentences.words: 1990年，霍法领导的国际卡车司机兄弟会正处于鼎盛时期，只有10%的政府工作人员属于工会；而现在，36%。

sentences.id: In 2009 the number of unionists in America’s public sector passed that of their fellow members in the private sector. (20 words)
sentences.en: 2009 年，美国公营部门中工会会员人数超过了私人企业。
sentences.cn: In Britain, more than half of public-sector workers but only about 15% of private-sector ones are unionized. (17 words)
sentences.words: 在英国，公营部门的员工有超过一半的都加入了工会，但私企的员工仅有约 15%。

sentences.id: There are three reasons for the public-sector unions’ thriving. (9 words)
sentences.en: 公营部门工会的兴起，原因有三。
sentences.cn: First, they can shut things down without suffering much in the way of consequences. (14 words)
sentences.words: 其一，他们可以罢工停业而无需承担太多后果。

sentences.id: Second, they are mostly bright and well-educated. (7 words)
sentences.en: 其二，他们大多数人都很聪明且受过良好教育。
sentences.cn: A quarter of America’s public-sector workers have a university degree. (10 words)
sentences.words: 美国公营部门四分之一的员工都有大学学历。

sentences.id: Third, they now dominate left-of-centre politics. (6 words)
sentences.en: 第三，他们目前在偏左派的政治中占主导地位。
sentences.cn: Some of their ties go back a long way. (9 words)
sentences.words: 他们与偏左派之间的某些联系由来已久。

sentences.id: Britain’s Labor Party, as its name implies, has long been associated with trade unionism. (14 words)
sentences.en: 英国的工党，顾名思义，与工会主义渊源久远。
sentences.cn: Its current leader, Ed Miliband, owes his position to votes from public-sector unions. (13 words)
sentences.words: 其现任领导，艾德·米利班德，就把自己党魁的席位归功于公营部门工会的选票。

sentences.id: At the state level their influence can be even more fearsome. (11 words)
sentences.en: 公营部门的工会在各州的影响领甚至更为可怕。
sentences.cn: Mark Baldassare of the Public Policy Institute of California points out that much of the state’s budget is patrolled by unions. (21 words)
sentences.words: 加州公共政策研究所的马克·巴尔达萨指出：加州大多数预算都受到工会的监察。

sentences.id: The teachers’ unions keep an eye on schools, the CCPOA on prisons and a variety of labor groups on health care. (21 words)
sentences.en: 教师工会监督学校，加州狱警协会监督监狱，还有各种劳工组织监督医保。
sentences.cn: In many rich countries average wages in the state sector are higher than in the private one. (17 words)
sentences.words: 在很多富裕国家，国营部门的平均工资要高于私企。

sentences.id: But the real gains come in benefits and work practices. (10 words)
sentences.en: 但实际的收益体现在福利和工作制度上。
sentences.cn: Politicians have repeatedly “backloaded” public-sector pay deals, keeping the pay increases modest but adding to holidays and especially pensions that are already generous. (23 words)
sentences.words: 政治家不断“变相提高”公营部门的薪水，工资增长幅度不大，但增加了假期，尤其是增加了养老金，本来就很多了。

sentences.id: Reform has been vigorously opposed, perhaps most notoriously in education, where charter schools, academies and merit pay all faced drawn-out battles. (21 words)
sentences.en: 改革受到强烈反对，最激烈的或许是在教育行业；其中，特许学校，私立学校，还有教师的绩效工资等问题都要面对旷日持久的战斗。
sentences.cn: Even though there is plenty of evidence that the quality of the teachers is the most important variable, teachers’ unions have fought against getting rid of bad ones and promoting good ones. (32 words)
sentences.words: 尽管大量事实表明，教师素质是最重要的变量，但教师工会仍然反对教师队伍中的优胜劣汰。

sentences.id: As the cost to everyone else has become clearer, politicians have begun to clamp down. (15 words)
sentences.en: 对其他任何人而言，付出的代价日益明显，政治家就开始打压。
sentences.cn: In Wisconsin the unions have rallied thousands of supporters against Scott Walker, the hardline Republican governor. (16 words)
sentences.words: 在威斯康辛，工会召集了上千上万的支持者，反对斯科特·沃克，他是共和党强硬派州长。

sentences.id: But many within the public sector suffer under the current system, too. (12 words)
sentences.en: 但是很多在公营部门的人在目前体制下也深受其害。
sentences.cn: John Donahue at Harvard’s Kennedy School points out that the norms of culture in Western civil services suit those who want to stay put but is bad for high achievers. (30 words)
sentences.words: 哈佛肯尼迪学院的约翰·唐纳修指出，西方行政部门的文化标准适合那些不求上进的人，但不利于那些追求卓越的人。

sentences.id: The only American public-sector workers who earn well above $250,000 a year are university sports coaches and the president of the United States. (23 words)
sentences.en: 美国公营部门的员工，年薪远超 25 万以上的，只有大学体育教练和美国总统。
sentences.cn: Bankers’ fat pay packets have attracted much criticism, but a public-sector system that does not reward high achievers may be a much bigger problem for America. (26 words)
sentences.words: 银行家厚实的工资袋饱受指责，但公营部门体制不奖励优秀人才，或许才是美国一个更大的问题。

sentences.id:
1301
In the 2006 film version of The Devil Wears Prada, Miranda Priestly, played by Meryl Streep, scolds her unattractive assistant for imagining that high fashion doesn’t affect her. (28 words)
sentences.en: 在2006年电影版的《穿普拉达的女王》中，米兰达·普里斯特利，由梅丽尔·斯特里普饰演，责备其助理衣着难看，因为后者认为高端时尚与己无关。
sentences.cn: Priestly explains how the deep blue color of the assistant’s sweater descended over the years from fashion shows to department stores and to the bargain bin in which the poor girl doubtless found her garment. (35 words)
sentences.words: 普里斯特利解释了她助手那件深蓝色毛衣，如何在几年时间就从时尚秀场上沦落到百货商店，最后到降价处理区——无疑，这个可怜的女孩就是在那里淘到衣服的。

sentences.id: This top-down conception of the fashion business couldn’t be more out of date or at odds with the feverish world described in Overdressed, Elizabeth Cline’s three-year indictment of “fast fashion”. (30 words)
sentences.en: 时尚行业这种自上而下的观点早已过时了，也与《时尚经济学》一书中描写的狂热世界迥异，该书是伊丽莎白·克莱因对“快时尚”的三年控诉。
sentences.cn: In the last decade or so, advances in technology have allowed mass-market labels such as Zara, H&M, and Uniqlo to react to trends more quickly and anticipate demand more precisely. (30 words)
sentences.words: 在过去的大约十年间，技术的进步已经使得大众服装品牌，如飒拉（Zara）、海恩斯莫里斯（H&M）、优衣库（Uniqlo）等，能够对时尚做出更快速的反应，对需求做出更精准的预测。

sentences.id: Quicker turnarounds mean less wasted inventory, more frequent releases, and more profit. (12 words)
sentences.en: 周转更快意味着库存浪费更少、新品发布更多、利润更高。
sentences.cn: These labels encourage style-conscious consumers to see clothes as disposable—meant to last only a wash or two, although they don’t advertise that—and to renew their wardrobe every few weeks. (31 words)
sentences.words: 这些品牌鼓励追求时尚的消费者把衣服当成是一次性用品——买来就是为只穿一两水，尽管他们没有这么宣传——他们还鼓励消费者每隔几周就更新一次衣柜。

sentences.id: By offering on-trend items at dirt-cheap prices, Cline argues, these brands have hijacked fashion cycles, shaking an industry long accustomed to a seasonal pace. (24 words)
sentences.en: 克莱因说，这些品牌以极其低廉的价格销售时髦的商品，控制了时尚的循环周期，撼动了整个行业，而这个行业曾长期以来习惯了季节更替的步伐。
sentences.cn: The victims of this revolution, of course, are not limited to designers. (12 words)
sentences.words: 这场变革的受害者，当然，不只是设计师。

sentences.id: For H&M to offer a $5.95 knit miniskirt in all its 2,300-plus stores around the world, it must rely on low-wage overseas labor, order in volumes that strain natural resources, and use massive amounts of harmful chemicals. (37 words)
sentences.en: 要在全世界其2300多家门店销售一款5.95美元的针织超短裙，海恩斯莫里斯（H&M）就必须要依靠海外廉价的劳动力，大批采购原材料以至于过度消耗自然资源，还大量使用有害的化学物质。
sentences.cn: Overdressed is the fashion world’s answer to consumer-activist bestsellers like Michael Pollan’s The Omnivore’s Dilemma. (15 words)
sentences.words: 如果说迈克尔·波伦的《杂食者的两难》是消费者激进分子的畅销书，那么《时尚经济学》则是时尚界的同类书籍。

sentences.id: “Mass-produced clothing, like fast food, fills a hunger and need, yet is non-durable and wasteful,” Cline argues. (17 words)
sentences.en: 批量生产的服装，像快餐，尽管满足了人们的渴望和需求，却不耐用，还浪费，”克莱因如是说。
sentences.cn: Americans, she finds, buy roughly 20 billion garments a year—about 64 items per person—and no matter how much they give away, this excess leads to waste. (28 words)
sentences.words: 她发现，美国人每年要买大约200亿件服装（每人约64件），且无论他们捐赠多少，这种无节制都会造成浪费。

sentences.id: Towards the end of Overdressed, Cline introduced her ideal, a Brooklyn woman named Sarah Kate Beaumont, who since 2008 has made all of her own clothes—and beautifully. (28 words)
sentences.en: 在《时尚经济学》的结尾，克莱因给大家介绍了她的典范，一位布鲁克林区的女士，名叫萨拉·凯特·博蒙特，她从2008年起所有衣服都自己做——相当漂亮。
sentences.cn: But as Cline is the first to note, it took Beaumont decades to perfect her craft; her example can’t be knocked off. (22 words)
sentences.words: 不过正如克莱因首次提到的，博蒙特花了几十年来完善自己的手艺，她的事例不能轻易复制。

sentences.id: Though several fast-fashion companies have made efforts to curb their impact on labor and the environment—including H&M, with its green Conscious Collection line—Cline believes lasting change can only be effected by the customer. (35 words)
sentences.en: 尽管几家快时尚公司已经在努力控制其对劳动力和环境的影响——包括海恩斯莫里斯（H&M），他们推行了环保的“可持续时尚”——但克莱因相信只有消费者才能促成持久的变革。
sentences.cn: She exhibits the idealism common to many advocates of sustainability, be it in food or in energy. (17 words)
sentences.words: 她表现了很多倡导可持续发展的人都具有的理想主义，无论是在食品还是在能源方面。

sentences.id: Vanity is a constant; people will only start shopping more sustainably when they can’t afford not to. (17 words)
sentences.en: 虚荣是一种常态，人们只有在无力支付非理性购物时，才会开始更加理性地购物。
sentences.cn:
1302
An old saying has it that half of all advertising budgets are wasted—the trouble is, no one knows which half. (21 words)
sentences.words: 老话说得好，打广告的钱一半都打了水漂——问题是，没人知道究竟是哪一半。

sentences.id: In the internet age, at least in theory, this fraction can be much reduced. (14 words)
sentences.en: 在互联网时代，至少在理论上，浪费的那部分可以大幅减少。
sentences.cn: By watching what people search for, click on and say online, companies can aim “behavioural” ads at those most likely to buy. (22 words)
sentences.words: 通过观察人们在网上搜索、点击、和发表的内容，企业可以针对最有可能购买的人投放“行为”广告。

sentences.id: In the past couple of weeks a quarrel has illustrated the value to advertisers of such fine-grained information: Should advertisers assume that people are happy to be tracked and sent behavioural ads? (32 words)
sentences.en: 在过去几周，一场争论已经说明了这种精细信息对于广告商的价值：广告商是否可以认为用户乐意被追踪且接受行为广告？
sentences.cn: Or should they have explicit permission? (6 words)
sentences.words: 还是说广告商是否应该得到用户明确的许可？

sentences.id: In December 2010 America’s Federal Trade Commission (FTC) proposed adding a “do not track” (DNT) option to internet browsers, so that users could tell advertisers that they did not want to be followed. (33 words)
sentences.en: 2010年12月，美国联邦贸易委员会（FTC）曾提出，应该在网络浏览器上添加“禁止追踪”（DNT）选项，这样用户就可以告诉广告商他们不想被追踪。
sentences.cn: Microsoft’s Internet Explorer and Apple’s Safari both offer DNT; Google’s Chrome is due to do so this year. (18 words)
sentences.words: 微软的IE浏览器和苹果公司的Safari目前都有“禁止追踪”选项；谷歌公司的酷容浏览器（Chrome）今年也会这么做。

sentences.id: In February the FTC and the Digital Advertising Alliance (DAA) agreed that the industry would get cracking on responding to DNT requests. (22 words)
sentences.en: 二月，联邦贸易委员会（FTC）和数字广告联盟（DAA）一直认为，该行业将会加快响应“禁止追踪”要求。
sentences.cn: On May 31st Microsoft set off the row: It said that Internet Explorer 10, the version due to appear with Windows 8, would have DNT as a default. (28 words)
sentences.words: 5月31日，微软就激起了争议：称其计划与视窗操作系统Windows 8 一同发布的IE10浏览器上，会把禁止追踪（DNT）设为默认选项。

sentences.id: Advertisers are horrified. (3 words)
sentences.en: 广告商惶恐不已。
sentences.cn: Human nature being what it is, most people stick with default settings. (12 words)
sentences.words: 人性使然，大多数用户都会坚持使用浏览器的默认设置。

sentences.id: Few switch DNT on now, but if tracking is off it will stay off. (14 words)
sentences.en: 现在很少有人会开启“禁止追踪”，但如果追踪处于关闭状态，它就会一直处于关闭状态。
sentences.cn: Bob Liodice, the chief executive of the Association of National Advertisers, says consumers will be worse off if the industry cannot collect information about their preferences. (26 words)
sentences.words: 美国广告协会会长鲍勃·利奥狄斯称，如果该行业不能收集用户偏好，消费者的处境更糟糕。

sentences.id: People will not get fewer ads, he says. (8 words)
sentences.en: 用户看到的广告不会减少，他说。
sentences.cn: “They’ll get less meaningful, less targeted ads.” (7 words)
sentences.words: “他们会看到更没有意义，更没有针对性的广告。”

sentences.id: It is not yet clear how advertisers will respond. (9 words)
sentences.en: 广告商作何反应，尚不得知。
sentences.cn: Getting a DNT signal does not oblige anyone to stop tracking, although some companies have promised to do so. (19 words)
sentences.words: 收到“禁止追踪”的信号并不会使任何公司停止追踪，尽管有些公司已经承诺如此。

sentences.id: Unable to tell whether someone really objects to behavioural ads or whether they are sticking with Microsoft’s default, some may ignore a DNT signal and press on anyway. (28 words)
sentences.en: 由于无法分辨有人是真的反对行为广告，还是他们保留了微软的默认设置，有些公司可能会忽视“禁止追踪”信号，一如既往继续追踪。
sentences.cn: Also unclear is why Microsoft has gone it alone. (9 words)
sentences.words: 同样不清楚的是，微软为什么要这样特立独行。

sentences.id: After all, it has an ad business too, which it says will comply with DNT requests, though it is still working out how. (23 words)
sentences.en: 毕竟，微软也有广告业务，它声称其广告业务也遵守“禁止追踪”的要求，尽管还在研究如何遵守。
sentences.cn: If it is trying to upset Google, which relies almost wholly on advertising, it has chosen an indirect method: there is no guarantee that DNT by default will become the norm. (31 words)
sentences.words: 如果微软是在试图挫败谷歌的话，后者几乎完全依赖广告，那么它选择了一种迂回的方法：无法保证将“禁止追踪”设置为默认的做法会成为行规。

sentences.id: DNT does not seem an obviously huge selling point for Windows 8—though the firm has compared some of its other products favourably with Google’s on that count before. (29 words)
sentences.en: “禁止追踪”似乎不是视窗操作系统（Windows）8的最大卖点——尽管微软的其他产品在这方面要比谷歌强。
sentences.cn: Brendon Lynch, Microsoft’s chief privacy officer, blogged: “We believe consumers should have more control.” (14 words)
sentences.words: 微软首席隐私官布兰登·林奇在博客中写到：“我们觉得消费者应该有更多的控制权。”

sentences.id: Could it really be that simple? (6 words)
sentences.en: 真那么简单吗？
sentences.cn:
1303
Up until a few decades ago, our visions of the future were largely—though by no means uniformly—glowingly positive. (20 words)
sentences.words: 几十年来，我们对于未来的看法，大体上说，尽管谈不上一致，也算是相当地乐观。

sentences.id: Science and technology would cure all the ills of humanity, leading to lives of fulfilment and opportunity for all. (19 words)
sentences.en: 科技或许会解决人类的所有问题，使每个人都觉得有作为，有前途。
sentences.cn: Now utopia has grown unfashionable, as we have gained a deeper appreciation of the range of threats facing us, from asteroid strike to epidemic flu and to climate change. (29 words)
sentences.words: 如今，空想已不合时宜，因为我们对自己面临的种种威胁有了更深刻的认识，无论是小行星撞击，还是流感，再到气候变化。

sentences.id: You might even be tempted to assume that humanity has little future to look forward to. (16 words)
sentences.en: 你甚至可能会认为，人类可能没什么盼头了。
sentences.cn: But such gloominess is misplaced. (5 words)
sentences.words: 但这样的担心没有必要。

sentences.id: The fossil record shows that many species have endured for millions of years—so why shouldn’t we? (17 words)
sentences.en: 化石记录表明，许多物种已经存活了数百万年之久——那么我们为什么就不可以呢？
sentences.cn: Take a broader look at our species’ place in the universe, and it becomes clear that we have an excellent chance of surviving for tens, if not hundreds, of thousands of years. (32 words)
sentences.words: 若以更广阔的视野来看看我们这个物种在宇宙中的地位，很显然，我们还是大有希望再活个几万年，如果说到不了几十万年的话。

sentences.id: Look up Homo sapiens in the “Red List” of threatened species of the International Union for the Conservation of Nature (IUCN) and you will read: “Listed as Least Concern as the species is very widely distributed, adaptable, currently increasing, and there are no major threats resulting in an overall population decline.” (51 words)
sentences.en: 如果查阅国际自然保护联盟濒危物种 《红色名录》上“智人”这一条目，你会读到：“列为无危级别，由于该物种分布非常广泛，适应性强，目前数量持续上升，无重大威胁会导致其整体数量下降”。
sentences.cn: So what does our deep future hold? (7 words)
sentences.words: 那么，我们的遥远未来会是什么样子呢？

sentences.id: A growing number of researchers and organizations are now thinking seriously about that question. (14 words)
sentences.en: 越来越多的研究人员和组织机构正在认真思考这个问题。
sentences.cn: For example, the Long Now Foundation has as its flagship project a mechanical clock that is designed to still be marking time thousands of years hence. (26 words)
sentences.words: 比如，恒今基金会（Long Now Foundation）有一个旗舰项目，是一台机械时钟，设计出来是为了能够在几千年后还能记录时间。

sentences.id: Perhaps willfully, it may be easier to think about such lengthy timescales than about the more immediate future. (18 words)
sentences.en: 或许有人一意孤行，推想久远的未来要比思考眼前的未来更为容易。
sentences.cn: The potential evolution of today’s technology, and its social consequences, is dazzlingly complicated, and it’s perhaps best left to science fiction writers and futurologists to explore the many possibilities we can envisage. (32 words)
sentences.words: 当今技术的发展潜力机器社会影响非常复杂，或许最好是让科幻小说家和未来学家去探索我们能想象到的种种可能。

sentences.id: That’s one reason why we have launched Arc, a new publication dedicated to the near future. (16 words)
sentences.en: 这就是我们发行《弧》这本新刊物的原因，它旨在探索不远的将来。
sentences.cn: But take a longer view and there is a surprising amount that we can say with considerable assurance. (18 words)
sentences.words: 但如果看得更远点，有相当大部分未来的事情，我们可以有很大的把握说清楚。

sentences.id: As so often, the past holds the key to the future: we have now identified enough of the long-term patterns shaping the history of the planet, and our species, to make evidence-based forecasts about the situations in which our descendants will find themselves. (43 words)
sentences.en: 人们常说，看过去，知未来：目前，我们已经充分认识到塑造地球，及我们人类发展史的长期模式，所以我们能够有根有据地预测到未来我们子孙后代的生存环境。
sentences.cn: This long perspective makes the pessimistic view of our prospects seem more likely to be a passing fad. (18 words)
sentences.words: 我们曾有一阵对人类的未来感到悲观，着眼未来，似乎瞬间化解了这样的悲观态度。

sentences.id: To be sure, the future is not all rosy. (9 words)
sentences.en: 诚然，未来绝非一片光明。
sentences.cn: But we are now knowledgeable enough to reduce many of the risks that threatened the existence of earlier humans, and to improve the lot of those to come. (28 words)
sentences.words: 不过，我们已经掌握了足够的知识，有能力去降低曾经威胁过早期人类生存的许多风险，改善后人的命运。

sentences.id:
1304
On a five to three vote, the Supreme Court knocked out much of Arizona’s immigration law Monday—a modest policy victory for the Obama Administration. (25 words)
sentences.en: 周一，最高法院以5：3的投票结果，裁定亚利桑那州移民法多项条款无效——对奥巴马政府来说，这是场小小的政策胜利。
sentences.cn: But on the more important matter of the Constitution, the decision was an 8-0 defeat for the Administration’s effort to upset the balance of power between the federal government and the states. (32 words)
sentences.words: 但在涉及宪法这一更重要的问题上，8：0的决议挫败了奥巴马政府想要打破联邦政府和各州之间权力平衡的努力。

sentences.id: In Arizona v. United States, the majority overturned three of the four contested provisions of Arizona’s controversial plan to have state and local police enforce federal immigration law. (28 words)
sentences.en: 在这场亚利桑那州对联邦政府的案例中，亚利桑那州计划要求州警察和地方警察执行联邦移民法，从而引起争议；该计划中有四项条款受到质疑，其中三项被最高法院多数票裁决无效。
sentences.cn: The Constitutional principles that Washington alone has the power to “establish a uniform Rule of Naturalization” and that federal laws precede state laws are noncontroversial. (25 words)
sentences.words: 宪法原则是：只有华府才有权“制定统一的归化条例”，联邦法律高于各州法律，这两点毋庸置疑。

sentences.id: Arizona had attempted to fashion state policies that ran parallel to the existing federal ones. (15 words)
sentences.en: 但亚利桑那很久以前就企图制定与现有联邦法并行的州政策。
sentences.cn: Justice Anthony Kennedy, joined by Chief Justice John Roberts and the Court’s liberals, ruled that the state flew too close to the federal sun. (24 words)
sentences.words: 大法官安东尼·肯尼迪，首席大法官约翰·罗伯茨，还有法庭的自由派等人裁定，亚利桑那州不知分寸，挑衅联邦政府。

sentences.id: On the overturned provisions the majority held Congress had deliberately “occupied the field” and Arizona had thus intruded on the federal’s privileged powers. (23 words)
sentences.en: 对于已裁决无效的三项条款，法官多数认为，国会早已有意“占地”，故亚利桑那州侵犯了联邦特权。
sentences.cn: However, the Justices said that Arizona police would be allowed to verify the legal status of people who come in contact with law enforcement. (24 words)
sentences.words: 但是，大法官们都认为，亚利桑那州警察有权核查可疑人员的法律身份。

sentences.id: That’s because Congress has always envisioned joint federal-state immigration enforcement and explicitly encourages state officers to share information and cooperate with federal colleagues. (23 words)
sentences.en: 这是因为国会一直期望联邦政府和州政府在移民政策上能联合执法，并明确鼓励州政府官员能与联邦同事之间能信息共享，通力合作。
sentences.cn: Two of the three objecting Justices—Samuel Alito and Clarence Thomas—agreed with this Constitutional logic but disagreed about which Arizona rules conflicted with the federal statute. (27 words)
sentences.words: 投反对票的三位大法官中，有两位，即塞缪尔·阿利托和克拉伦斯·托马斯赞成这一宪法逻辑，但对于亚利桑那州哪些法律与联邦法律相抵触，他们却存在分歧。

sentences.id: The only major objection came from Justice Antonin Scalia, who offered an even more robust defense of state privileges going back to the Alien and Sedition Acts. (27 words)
sentences.en: 唯一的主要反对来自安东宁·斯卡利亚大法官，他根据早期的《客籍法和惩治叛乱法》，强烈支持州政府特权。
sentences.cn: The 8-0 objection to President Obama turns on what Justice Samuel Alito describes in his objection as “a shocking assertion of federal executive power”. (24 words)
sentences.words: 以8: 0反对奥巴马总统的原因，如塞缪尔·阿利托在其反对意见中所说，在于其“太过于维护联邦行政权，令人震惊”。

sentences.id: The White House argued that Arizona’s laws conflicted with its enforcement priorities, even if state laws complied with federal statutes to the letter. (23 words)
sentences.en: 白宫辩称，亚利桑那州法律与联邦政府的执法优先权相抵触，即使州法律是在一字一句严格执行联邦法律。
sentences.cn: In effect, the White House claimed that it could invalidate any otherwise legitimate state law that it disagrees with. (19 words)
sentences.words: 事实上，白宫宣称，它可以否决任何合法但它不认同的州级法律。

sentences.id: Some powers do belong exclusively to the federal government, and control of citizenship and the borders is among them. (19 words)
sentences.en: 有些权利确实只属于联邦政府，其中包括公民身份控制权，边境管理权。
sentences.cn: But if Congress wanted to prevent states from using their own resources to check immigration status, it could. (18 words)
sentences.words: 但是，如果国会想阻止各州利用自己的资源核查移民身份，它能。

sentences.id: It never did so. (4 words)
sentences.en: 但从未如此。
sentences.cn: The Administration was in essence asserting that because it didn’t want to carry out Congress’s immigration wishes, no state should be allowed to do so either. (26 words)
sentences.words: 奥巴马政府实质上是想认定，因为自己不想执行国会在移民事务上的意愿，也不允许任何州政府去执行。

sentences.id: Every Justice rightly rejected this remarkable claim. (7 words)
sentences.en: 对于这一骇人听闻的要求，每一个大法官都拒绝了，合情合理。
sentences.cn:
1401
In order to “change lives for the better” and reduce “dependency”, George Osborne, Chancellor of the Exchequer, introduced the “upfront work search” scheme. (23 words)
sentences.words: 为了“改善生活”，减少“依赖”，财政大臣乔治·奥斯本提出了“找工作预付金”方案。

sentences.id: Only if the jobless arrive at the jobcentre with a CV, register for online job search, and start looking for work will they be eligible for benefit—and then they should report weekly rather than fortnightly. (36 words)
sentences.en: 失业者只有带简历到就业中心，上网注册求职，开始找工作，才有资格得到救济金；然后，他们还应该要每周汇报一次，而不是两周。
sentences.cn: What could be more reasonable? (5 words)
sentences.words: 还有什么比这更合理呢？

sentences.id: More apparent reasonableness followed. (4 words)
sentences.en: 接下来还有貌似更合理的。
sentences.cn: There will now be a seven-day wait for the jobseeker’s allowance. (11 words)
sentences.words: 现在，求职者要拿到津贴，得等七天。

sentences.id: “Those first few days should be spent looking for work, not looking to sign on,” he claimed. (17 words)
sentences.en: “头几天应该用来找工作，而不是指望办理失业登记”，他说。
sentences.cn: “We’re doing these things because we know they help people stay off benefits and help those on benefits get into work faster.” (22 words)
sentences.words: 我们做这些事情，是因为我们知道这样做能帮助人们不必靠救济金生活，并帮助那些依靠救济金生活的人更快地找到工作。”

sentences.id: Help? (1 words)
sentences.en: 帮助？
sentences.cn: Really? (1 words)
sentences.words: 真的？

sentences.id: On first hearing, this was the socially concerned chancellor, trying to change lives for the better, complete with “reforms” to an obviously indulgent system that demands too little effort from the newly unemployed to find work, and subsidises laziness. (39 words)
sentences.en: 乍一听，这是一位心系社会的财相啊，他努力让人民生活更美好，还要“改革”一个特别纵容的社会体制，因为这个体制几乎不要求新失业的人去努力找工作，还助长惰性。
sentences.cn: What motivated him, we were to understand, was his zeal for “fundamental fairness”—protecting the taxpayer, controlling spending and ensuring that only the most deserving claimants received their benefits. (29 words)
sentences.words: 不过我们要明白， 驱使他的是他热衷于“基本公平”——保护纳税人，控制开支，确保只有最值得帮助的申请人得到救济金。

sentences.id: Losing a job is hurting: you don’t skip down to the jobcentre with a song in your heart, delighted at the prospect of doubling your income from the generous state. (30 words)
sentences.en: 失去工作会让人难过：你不可能是心里哼着曲儿，蹦蹦跳跳地去就业中心，想着从慷慨的政府那里能拿到双倍的收入还挺开心。
sentences.cn: It is financially terrifying, psychologically embarrassing and you know that support is minimal and extraordinarily hard to get. (18 words)
sentences.words: 失业带来的是经济上的恐慌，心理上的尴尬，而且你知道失业津贴少得可怜，也极难获得。

sentences.id: You are now not wanted; you are now excluded from the work environment that offers purpose and structure in your life. (21 words)
sentences.en: 现在，没有工作单位要你；能给你生活带来奋斗目标和秩序的工作环境，你现在也参与不进去。
sentences.cn: Worse, the crucial income to feed yourself and your family and pay the bills has disappeared. (16 words)
sentences.words: 更糟糕的是，用来养活自己和家人，用来支付账单的主要收入来源，没有了。

sentences.id: Ask anyone newly unemployed what they want and the answer is always: a job. (14 words)
sentences.en: 如果问任何一个刚下岗的人想要什么；回答永远是：一份工作。
